description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched gable roof with deep overhangs and exposed rafters, characteristic of early 20th-century American residential architecture. The front facade is defined by a prominent covered porch supported by sturdy columns, accessed by a central staircase. The structure is elevated on short piers, with visible steps leading up to the porch on both the front and rear sides. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, contrasting with the darker, textured roofing material. Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front, with additional windows on the sides, providing ample natural light. The surrounding area is a simple, flat green lawn, suggesting a suburban or neighborhood setting.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide gable roof, deep porch, and symmetrical windows, set on a raised platform with steps leading to the entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central staircase, covered porch with columns, and symmetrical windows flanking the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended roof overhang, three small windows beneath the eaves, and the raised foundation.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary entrance with steps, a smaller covered porch, and fewer windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, with visible steps and the same window arrangement under the roof.
